gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 approved the release-evidence ownership move with no blocking findings. The review confirmed that the document module owns the implementation, the normalized AST matches the old legacy implementation after package-prefix rewrite, the legacy facade preserves top-level and star-import compatibility, legacy main keeps monkeypatchable globals local, release-bundle imports still resolve, and the document module has no direct restaurant_kv_serving imports.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_release_evidence.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q
repository secret-pattern scan across docs, source, tests, databricks, pr-evidence, and GitHub metadata
PYTHONPATH=src python import-boundary probe for document and legacy release_evidence modules
what_changed: Moved release evidence validation implementation into document_kv_cache.release_evidence with a curated document __all__ surface.
Replaced restaurant_kv_serving.release_evidence with a compatibility facade that preserves the broad legacy import surface and owns a local main function.
Added regressions for document and legacy CLI serializer monkeypatch behavior, public module ownership, legacy facade star-import compatibility, and explicit legacy-test governance.
why: Release evidence is part of the open-source package release gate, so its implementation should live in the document package while legacy restaurant imports continue to work during migration.
